iterations.hypothesis: USDC (listing 1) and USDE (listing 28) spread is mean-reverting; tick-based EWMA z-score with alpha=0.99, entry_z=1.0, close_z=0.2, max_hold=60s.
changes: Initial strategy: StablecoinStatArb based on NExchangeArb, entry_z=1.0, close_z=0.2, ewma_alpha=0.99, max_hold=60s
iterations.analysis: 90k+ fills in 3 days. Tick-based EWMA alpha=0.99 has half-life of only 22s at 3.16 ticks/sec. The std is tiny (tick-level noise ~0.22 bps), so z=1.0 fires constantly on noise. pct_positive_buckets=0.0: EWMA mean catches up to persistent spread, close fires when mean meets spread (not when spread reverts). Systematic losses.
iterations.next_action: Increase entry_z to 2.5, set close_z=0.0 to exit only when spread crosses mean.

iterations.hypothesis: Higher entry_z=2.5 filters noise; close_z=0.0 ensures exit only on true mean crossing; max_hold 10min.
iterations.analysis: 79% PnL improvement but still losing. Root cause confirmed: with tick-based EWMA, z=2.5 still fires at only ~0.55 bps above the mean because tick-level std is ~0.22 bps. close_z=0.0 helps but EWMA still adapts in 22s so z crosses 0 quickly due to mean drift, not spread reversion. Security 28 bought back at higher price after entry → spread widened.
iterations.next_action: Replace tick-based EWMA with time-weighted EWMA (halflife=300s). Lock entry reference (mean/std) at entry time so close is based on actual spread reversion, not EWMA adaptation.

iterations.hypothesis: Time-weighted EWMA (halflife=300s) gives a meaningful z-score reflecting genuine 5-min scale deviations. Locked entry reference prevents false closes when EWMA mean drifts up to meet persistent spread. entry_z=2.5, close_z=0.0, max_hold=10min.
changes: Added _locked_mean/_locked_std: store EWMA state at entry, use for all close z-score computations
iterations.analysis: Breakthrough: PnL +4.175, pct_positive_buckets=1.0. The 300s time-weighted EWMA correctly computes z-scores against a 5-minute scale baseline, making z=2.5 correspond to genuine spread anomalies. Locked entry reference ensures we close only when the spread truly reverts below the pre-entry mean, not when the EWMA drifts up. 1716 round trips at +0.00243 each. Security 1 profits (sell>buy by 6.21) are partially offset by Security 28 losses (buy>sell by 3.74), but net is positive. Latency insensitive: 2x and 0.5x latency produce identical results, as expected for a medium-term strategy. Queue model already risk_averse (skip test).
